title: Wombat Excrement
fact: Wombats are the only known animal in the world that produces cube-shaped feces, which helps prevent the droppings from rolling away when they mark their territory on rocks and logs.
context: Their intestines have varying elastic properties that mold the shape.

title: The Eiffel Tower's Growth
fact: The Eiffel Tower can grow up to 15 centimeters taller during the summer due to thermal expansion, as the iron structure heats up and expands in the sun.
context: Iron reacts to temperature changes by increasing its volume.

title: Cloud Weight
fact: A typical cumulus cloud—those fluffy, white ones seen on a sunny day—can weigh about 1.1 million pounds, which is roughly equivalent to the weight of 100 elephants.
context: Water droplets are spread over a massive cubic volume.

title: Sloth Digestion
fact: Sloths have one of the slowest digestive systems in the animal kingdom, often taking up to 30 days to fully digest a single leaf, which contributes to their low energy lifestyle.
context: Their metabolic rate is extremely low to conserve energy.

title: Neptune's Winds
fact: Neptune experiences the fastest planetary winds in the solar system, with speeds reaching over 1,200 miles per hour, making the surface conditions incredibly volatile and supersonic.
context: Internal heat and low friction drive these massive storms.
